guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable section behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when semantic project markup and CSS already provide the structure without reusable behavior.
guidance.responsive: Preserve semantic order and allow project CSS to adapt layout without changing the component contract.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable visually hidden behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when semantic project markup and CSS already provide the structure without reusable behavior.
guidance.responsive: Preserve semantic order and allow project CSS to adapt layout without changing the component contract.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable button behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when another Composer action primitive more accurately communicates the interaction.
guidance.responsive: Maintain an accessible target size and allow labels or groups to wrap without clipping.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable button link behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when another Composer action primitive more accurately communicates the interaction.
guidance.responsive: Maintain an accessible target size and allow labels or groups to wrap without clipping.

actions.description: Action objects rendered by ActionGroup as buttons or links.

onAction.description: Receives the selected ActionDescriptor for actions without href.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable action group behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when another Composer action primitive more accurately communicates the interaction.
guidance.responsive: Maintain an accessible target size and allow labels or groups to wrap without clipping.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable field behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable input behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable textarea behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable checkbox behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

options.description: Choice descriptors with label, value, optional description, and disabled state.

name.description: Native form field name; the selected value is included in FormData.

error.description: Visible error text associated with the radio group.

selectionHints.message: Use RadioGroup for a submitted single-choice form field; SegmentedControl is intended for navigation or mode selection.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable radio group behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable toggle behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the interaction is not a user choice or does not need selection and form semantics.
guidance.responsive: Allow choices to wrap or stack while preserving their native group semantics.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable badge behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the message is ordinary page content rather than state or outcome feedback.
guidance.responsive: Allow long and translated messages to wrap without separating them from related actions.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable callout behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the message is ordinary page content rather than state or outcome feedback.
guidance.responsive: Allow long and translated messages to wrap without separating them from related actions.

icon.description: Rendered decorative content, not an icon descriptor.

actions.description: Rendered React content. For descriptor actions, pass an ActionGroup element rather than the descriptor array.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable empty state behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the message is ordinary page content rather than state or outcome feedback.
guidance.responsive: Allow long and translated messages to wrap without separating them from related actions.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable spinner behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when no asynchronous or transitional state needs to be communicated.
guidance.responsive: Keep status text perceivable and respect reduced-motion preferences at every viewport.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable skeleton behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when no asynchronous or transitional state needs to be communicated.
guidance.responsive: Keep status text perceivable and respect reduced-motion preferences at every viewport.

guidance.use: Use for interruptive content that must move focus into a named modal surface and keep keyboard interaction contained until dismissal.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for content that can remain in document flow, lightweight anchored help, destructive confirmation, or a persistent side task.
guidance.responsive: Keep the content region within the viewport, preserve the close control, and verify trap, Escape, and focus restoration at narrow widths.

decision.nativeAlternative: A native dialog element can be sufficient when the project owns complete focus, dismissal, inertness, and restoration behavior.
decision.complexAlternative: Use Drawer for a persistent edge task or AlertDialog for a destructive decision that requires an explicit response.
decision.stateModel: Supports controlled open/onOpenChange and uncontrolled defaultOpen state; the trigger is required by the 0.6 contract.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required because Radix owns focus, portal, dismissal, and controlled open state.
decision.serialization: No value serialization; actions and submitted values remain project-owned.
decision.focusOwnership: Composer traps focus and returns it to the trigger; the project must provide a meaningful trigger and deliberate initial interactive content.
failureModes: Opening without a meaningful restoration target leaves keyboard users in an ambiguous location.
Using Dialog for non-modal content creates unnecessary focus interruption.
Placing an independently modal surface inside the dialog can create competing focus owners.

trigger.description: One rendered trigger element used as the focus-restoration target by the 0.6 contract.

open.description: Controlled visibility. Pair with onOpenChange; defaultOpen is ignored after controlled state is supplied.

actions.description: Rendered project-owned actions; Composer does not infer submit or mutation behavior.

runtime.listenerModel: Radix manages document-level focus, Escape, outside interaction, and trigger restoration while open.
runtime.nativeAlternative: Native dialog with project-owned focus and restoration behavior.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Use one active modal owner at a time; do not render large repeated collections of dialogs.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable tooltip behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content belongs in normal document flow or does not require managed focus and dismissal.
guidance.responsive: Keep content within the viewport while preserving focus trapping, dismissal, and touch access.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable tabs behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native link or a simpler Composer navigation primitive fully expresses the navigation model.
guidance.responsive: Keep every destination and control keyboard and touch accessible at narrow widths.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable breadcrumbs behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native link or a simpler Composer navigation primitive fully expresses the navigation model.
guidance.responsive: Keep every destination and control keyboard and touch accessible at narrow widths.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable pagination behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native link or a simpler Composer navigation primitive fully expresses the navigation model.
guidance.responsive: Keep every destination and control keyboard and touch accessible at narrow widths.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable menu behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native link or a simpler Composer navigation primitive fully expresses the navigation model.
guidance.responsive: Keep every destination and control keyboard and touch accessible at narrow widths.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable popover behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content belongs in normal document flow or does not require managed focus and dismissal.
guidance.responsive: Keep content within the viewport while preserving focus trapping, dismissal, and touch access.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable drawer behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content belongs in normal document flow or does not require managed focus and dismissal.
guidance.responsive: Keep content within the viewport while preserving focus trapping, dismissal, and touch access.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable toast behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the message is ordinary page content rather than state or outcome feedback.
guidance.responsive: Allow long and translated messages to wrap without separating them from related actions.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable disclosure behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content must remain visible or the interaction belongs in a modal workflow.
guidance.responsive: Keep triggers visible and expanded content within the viewport at narrow widths.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable accordion behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content must remain visible or the interaction belongs in a modal workflow.
guidance.responsive: Keep triggers visible and expanded content within the viewport at narrow widths.

fields.description: Field descriptors. Custom controls that render their own label, description, and error must set selfLabeled: true and own serialization.

actions.description: Rendered project-owned content placed before the submit button.

errors.description: External messages keyed by descriptor name; supplied values override internal server-result messages for the same field.

onSubmit.description: Receives native FormData and the form event. It may return field errors and a message, but server validation and mutation authority stay outside Composer.

guidance.use: Use for descriptor-driven forms whose field rendering, pending protection, server error display, and dependent visibility benefit from one shared contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when native semantic markup is clearer, when a form library already owns field registration, or when business-specific workflows do not fit descriptor fields.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, descriptions, validation links, controls, and submit actions readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

decision.nativeAlternative: Use a native form with local Composer field wrappers when descriptors add indirection without reuse.
decision.complexAlternative: Keep schema-driven, multi-step, autosaving, or server-action-specific orchestration in a project form layer that composes Composer controls.
decision.stateModel: Field defaults initialize local FormData-derived state; external errors and submitting remain authoritative when supplied.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for descriptor callbacks, dependency evaluation, error clearing, and pending submission state.
decision.serialization: Submits native FormData; custom self-labeled controls must own a stable name and native or hidden serialization contract.
decision.focusOwnership: Individual controls own focus; the project or FormSummary must move focus to actionable validation feedback after submission.
failureModes: Passing descriptor callbacks across a Server Component boundary fails serialization.
Custom controls without selfLabeled or a native name duplicate labels or disappear from FormData.
Treating client errors as authoritative permits invalid or unauthorized server mutations.

runtime.listenerModel: One form-level change, reset, and optional submit handler coordinate field state and pending protection.
runtime.nativeAlternative: Native form markup with local Composer controls.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Prefer focused forms; split very large conditional workflows into project-owned steps rather than hundreds of descriptors.

security.htmlPolicy: React content is escaped by default; rendered custom controls and serverError nodes remain project-owned.
security.clientValidation: Client errors, required state, honeypots, and disabled buttons are user experience aids, not a security boundary.
security.serverValidation: Authenticate and authorize the mutation, validate every FormData field, normalize values, reject unexpected fields, and apply CSRF protections appropriate to the application.
security.secretPolicy: Never place credentials, provider secrets, private keys, or privileged tokens in field descriptors, rendered errors, or client callbacks.
security.externalNavigation: Composer performs no redirect; validate any project-owned post-submit destination.
security.commerceAuthority: Pricing, inventory, tax, discount, payment, and order state must be recomputed and authorized on the server.
warnings: A successful client callback does not prove the server accepted or authorized a mutation.
Returned exception messages can expose server details if project callbacks pass them through unchanged.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable search select behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for display-only values or when a native control already satisfies the complete project contract.
guidance.responsive: Keep labels, errors, and controls readable at narrow widths and 200 percent zoom.

guidance.use: Use for an accessible native file picker plus drag-and-drop selection when the project owns upload transport, storage, scanning, and final validation.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when the project needs direct-to-provider transport, resumable uploads, media-library selection, or an import preview workflow.
guidance.responsive: Keep the native picker reachable without drag input, allow filenames and errors to wrap, and verify touch operation.

decision.nativeAlternative: A labeled native file input is the simplest option when drag-and-drop and selected-file presentation are unnecessary.
decision.complexAlternative: Use MediaField with MediaPicker or a project upload adapter when selection, existing assets, progress, retries, or storage policy are required.
decision.stateModel: Native file selection is uncontrolled; onFilesChange reports the current File list after client-side filtering.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for drag state and File objects, which cannot cross a Server Component boundary as serializable props.
decision.serialization: The native input can submit File values through FormData; File objects must not be serialized into JSON.
decision.focusOwnership: The native input owns keyboard focus; drop interactions must never be the only way to choose a file.
failureModes: Relying on accept or maxSize as security validation permits spoofed or oversized uploads.
Reading files eagerly in the browser can create memory pressure for large selections.
Removing the native picker makes the control inaccessible to keyboard-only users.

accept.description: Native picker hint only. It narrows the chooser but does not validate MIME type, extension, or file contents.

maxSize.description: Client-side byte threshold for feedback only; repeat size and content validation on the server or upload service.

onFilesChange.description: Receives browser File objects after selection; the project owns transport, cancellation, scanning, and persistence.

runtime.listenerModel: Native input change plus drag enter, leave, over, and drop handlers on the root.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Keep selection counts and browser memory bounded; use a project upload queue for many or large files.

security.htmlPolicy: Labels, descriptions, filenames, and errors render as escaped React content.
security.clientValidation: accept and maxSize are chooser and feedback constraints only; filenames and browser MIME values are untrusted.
security.serverValidation: Enforce authenticated authorization, size limits, content signatures, allowed media types, malware scanning, safe storage keys, and download headers on the server.
security.secretPolicy: Never pass storage credentials, signed provider secrets, or privileged upload tokens through wrapper props or evidence.
security.externalNavigation: Composer does not navigate or create download URLs.
warnings: Frontend validation does not make an upload safe.
File names, MIME types, dimensions, and embedded metadata can be attacker-controlled.

doctorHints.message: Declare an accept policy for FileUpload, then enforce type, size, and file-content validation again on the server; accept is only a chooser hint.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable detail rows behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable list view behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use for a semantic table with a bounded row set, visible caption, project-rendered cells, and optional local column sorting.
guidance.avoid: Avoid for virtualized collections, column resizing or pinning, bulk selection, spreadsheet interaction, or card-like content that is not tabular.
guidance.responsive: Preserve table semantics and contain intentional horizontal overflow without changing logical reading order.

decision.nativeAlternative: A native table is simpler when the project does not need descriptor columns or Composer sorting state.
decision.complexAlternative: Use DataGrid for application-scale selection, pinning, resizing, controlled remote state, or virtualization.
decision.stateModel: Supports controlled sort/onSortChange and uncontrolled defaultSort; rows and column descriptors remain caller-owned.
decision.serverClientBoundary: Client boundary required for local sorting and descriptor cell render functions.
decision.serialization: Rows and sort descriptors must be serializable before entering a client boundary; cell render callbacks must be declared in client code.
decision.focusOwnership: Sortable header buttons own focus; project-rendered interactive cells must maintain sensible tab order.
failureModes: Using DataTable for non-tabular cards creates misleading row and column semantics.
Passing cell callbacks from a Server Component fails serialization.
Rendering an unbounded row set causes slow updates and unusable narrow-screen overflow.

rows.description: A bounded caller-owned row array with stable identifiers.

columns.description: Column descriptors; custom cell render functions must be created inside a client boundary.

sort.description: Controlled sort descriptor. Pair with onSortChange; defaultSort initializes only uncontrolled state.

runtime.listenerModel: Interactive sort buttons only; no global listeners or portal.
runtime.scaleGuidance: Use for bounded local datasets; move to pagination, server querying, or DataGrid before row counts become application-scale.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable filter bar behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable master detail layout behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when project-specific business behavior would make the shared data contract misleading.
guidance.responsive: Preserve reading order and keyboard access; contain intentional overflow inside the component boundary.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable resource boundary behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when no asynchronous or transitional state needs to be communicated.
guidance.responsive: Keep status text perceivable and respect reduced-motion preferences at every viewport.

value.description: Caller-managed progress in the same units as max; omit for indeterminate progress. Composer does not observe scroll or workflow state.

max.description: Upper bound for value, defaulting to 100.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable progress bar behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when no asynchronous or transitional state needs to be communicated.
guidance.responsive: Keep status text perceivable and respect reduced-motion preferences at every viewport.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable command menu behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when content belongs in normal document flow or does not require managed focus and dismissal.
guidance.responsive: Keep content within the viewport while preserving focus trapping, dismissal, and touch access.

slides.description: A bounded set of independently navigable content slides; not a site-wide horizontal narrative or page transition engine.

guidance.use: Use when a project needs reusable carousel behavior with the registered state and accessibility contract.
guidance.avoid: Avoid when a native link or a simpler Composer navigation primitive fully expresses the navigation model.
guidance.responsive: Keep every destination and control keyboard and touch accessible at narrow widths.
